Interface EqualsModProperty<T>

Type Parameters:
T - the type of the objects that are checked for equality or hashed

TimesAssignment, TransactionStatement, Transient, Try, TwoState, TypeCast, TypeDeclaration, TypeOf, TypeOperator, TypeRef, TypeReferenceImp, Unpack, UnsignedShiftRight, UnsignedShiftRightAssignment, UnwindLoop, VariableDeclaration, VariableNamer.IndProgramElementName, VariableReference, VariableSpecification, VisibilityModifier, Volatile, While
public interface EqualsModProperty<T>
Interface to check for equality ignoring given properties and to compute according hash codes.

Overview of currently implemented properties that can be used with this interface:

  • For Terms:
    1. RenamingTermProperty: Terms are checked for equality modulo renaming.
    2. TermLabelsProperty: Terms are checked for equality ignoring all term labels.
    3. IrrelevantTermLabelsProperty: Terms are checked for equality ignoring irrelevant term labels.
    4. ProofIrrelevancyProperty: Terms are checked for equality ignoring proof irrelevant attributes.
  • For SourceElements:
    1. RenamingSourceElementProperty: Source elements are checked for equality modulo renaming.

    • Method Summary

      Modifier and Type
      Method
      Description
      <V> boolean
      equalsModProperty(Object o, Property<T> property, V... v)
      Checks whether this object is equal to o modulo the property described by property.
      int
      hashCodeModProperty(Property<? super T> property)
      Computes the hash code according to the given ignored property.

    • Method Details

      • equalsModProperty

        <V> boolean equalsModProperty(Object o, Property<T> property, V... v)
        Checks whether this object is equal to o modulo the property described by property.

        Some properties call for additional arguments. For more information on that, see the documentation of equalsModThisProperty for the certain property.

        To get an overview of the currently implemented properties, see the documentation of EqualsModProperty.

        Type Parameters:
        V - the type of the additional parameters needed by property for the comparison
        Parameters:
        o - the object that is checked for equality
        property - the property to be ignored in the equality check
        v - the additional arguments needed by property for the equality check
        Returns:
        whether this object is equal to o

      • hashCodeModProperty

        int hashCodeModProperty(Property<? super T> property)
        Computes the hash code according to the given ignored property.
        Parameters:
        property - the ignored property according to which the hash code is computed
        Returns:
        the hash code of this object
